title: System Design Interview — An Insider’s Guide
subtitle: Volume 2
author: Alex Xu, Sahn Lam
description: A sequel covering 13 advanced system design interview questions with a 4-step framework, 300+ diagrams, and deep dives into real-world distributed systems — from proximity services and payment engines to stock exchanges and S3-like object storage.
keyIdeas: CQRS and Event Sourcing in Payments
Payment System Design and Digital Wallets
keyTakeaways: Start every design with a 4-step framework: requirements, estimation, high-level design, deep dive.
Use back-of-the-envelope calculations (QPS, storage, bandwidth) to scope the system before architecture.
Geohashing is the foundation for proximity services, nearby friends, and mapping systems.
Distributed message queues require a balance of ordering guarantees, partitioning, and exactly-once delivery.
Metrics monitoring pipelines use time-series databases with downsampling and alerting rules.
Ad click aggregation demands real-time streaming with windowed counts and rollup strategies.
Hotel reservation systems must handle race conditions with pessimistic locking or optimistic concurrency.
Payment systems need idempotency keys, two-phase commits, and compensation transactions (Saga pattern).
Digital wallets require append-only ledgers with event sourcing for auditability.
Stock exchange design centers on an in-memory order book with price-time priority and risk controls.
Identify bottlenecks first, then optimize — do not prematurely optimize the happy path.
whoShouldRead: Software engineers preparing for senior/staff-level system design interviews at FAANG and equivalent
Backend developers who want a structured vocabulary for discussing distributed system trade-offs
Engineers looking to transition from feature development to architecture roles
Readers of Volume 1 who want more advanced case studies on payments, real-time systems, and storage

relatedBooks.title: System Design Interview — An Insider’s Guide, Volume 1
